fixture.description: A 64-bit id string from an upstream system is parsed with Number(), losing precision for values above 2^53, so large ids become wrong or collide.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that parseAccountId converts a 64-bit id string to a JavaScript number via Number(), which loses precision above 2^53 (Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) — so large account ids are silently rounded and can become wrong or collide. It should recommend keeping the id as a string (or using BigInt). Severity IMPORTANT or DEBT. FAIL if the precision-loss for large integers is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags Number() precision loss for 64-bit ids above 2^53
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim Number() is always wrong (it is the 64-bit id context that matters)
Does NOT require a failing example value to flag it
